question_frequency.rationale: The Pair attempted one didactic question (A vs B for encode), but immediately abandoned it claiming the 'pairing tool isn't available' and proceeded unilaterally. No further questions were posed at other decision points (decode approach, regex generality, multi-digit handling).

question_quality.rationale: The single offered choice — groupby vs manual accumulator loop — is a genuinely substantive Pythonic-vs-explicit trade-off that hits a real teachable distinction. However, decode (where the parsing approach has more interesting trade-offs around multi-digit counts) was never offered as a choice.

scaffolding.rationale: Only one choice was presented before the Pair bailed and wrote everything itself, so there's no real progression. The post-hoc commentary on groupby gotchas and regex generality is informative but not scaffolded learning.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the closing notes are useful and actionable, but the flow is broken by the Pair's mid-turn abandonment of its own option-presentation framework with a confusing 'tool isn't available' aside. The user never got to actually choose.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code works: all five round-trip tests pass, groupby + regex implementation is clean and correct. Comparable to baseline, though baseline tested 7 cases vs experiment's 5 and explicitly handled malformed input with ValueError.

friction.rationale: Experiment took 399s vs baseline's 54s (~7x longer) for a roughly equivalent solution. The pedagogical payoff is modest — one aborted choice and some post-hoc notes — which doesn't justify the time multiplier.
